commit.message:
fix: update download stuck at 0% + reopen update bar

Root cause: when auto-download completes before user clicks Check for
Update, the update-available event resets progress to 0% but no
download-progress or update-downloaded events fire (already done).

Fix: Track update state (idle/downloading/downloaded) in main process.
check-for-update handler returns cached state instead of re-checking.
Renderer shows 'ready to install' immediately for downloaded updates.

Also: dismissing the update bar now converts the Check for Update
button into a pulsing 'Restart & Update' button so user can reopen
or install the update at any time.

Bumps version to 0.3.4.

fix(config): Fix reports.register() failing with "Cannot call TaskOutputs.file() after task started execution" (#1618) (#1648)

* Initial plan

* fix: resolve reports.register() failing with TaskOutputs.file() error during execution

When using reports.register() for lazy report registration, the domain objects
are realized at iteration time. Previously, the factory function called
(outputs as TaskOutputs).file() which fails if called during task execution.

Fix: remove the outputs.file() call from the factory and instead register all
required reports' output files via a lazy provider in the init block. The
provider is evaluated at task-graph finalization (before execution), not during
the task action itself.

Also adds a functional test covering the exact scenario from issue #1539:
registering multiple reports with register() without explicit required=true.

Fixes #1539



* fix: correct output-tracking for lazily-registered reports

The previous approach had two bugs caught by CI:

1. ArchUnit violation: outputs.files() without explicit cast caused Kotlin
   to infer AbstractTask.getOutputs() return type as TaskOutputsInternal
   (internal Gradle API). Fix: cast to org.gradle.api.tasks.TaskOutputs.

2. Lazy items excluded from output tracking: Kotlin's Collection.filter{}
   calls Iterable.iterator() which only returns already-realized items.
   Items registered with reports.register() stay pending/lazy and are
   invisible to iterator(), so the provider returned an empty file list.
   Fix: use reports.matching{} (Gradle's API) which realizes pending items
   when the resulting collection is iterated.

Also uses an anonymous Callable object instead of a lambda, following the
existing NamedDomainObjectFactory pattern for configuration-cache safety.

Fixes #1539

* Initial plan



* fix: pre-realize pending reports to prevent config-cache ConcurrentModificationException

When Gradle serializes the task state for the configuration cache, it
visits the reports container's internal pending map. If any reports were
registered lazily via reports.register(), the serializer encounters
ProviderInternal objects in that map; realizing those providers moves
entries out of the map while the serializer is still iterating it,
causing a ConcurrentModificationException.

Fix: after executing the user's configuration action inside reports{},
call reports.toList() to force-realize every pending item. This empties
the pending map before the configuration-cache serializer ever visits it,
so the ConcurrentModificationException no longer occurs.

Fixes #1539

commit.message:
fix(velero-ui): increase probe delays and upgrade timeout

NestJS app takes 60-90s to start; with the default 60s liveness probe
initialDelaySeconds the container was killed before binding to port 3000,
causing an endless CrashLoopBackOff / Helm rollback cycle.

- livenessProbe.initialDelaySeconds: 60 → 120
- readinessProbe.initialDelaySeconds: 30 → 60
- HelmRelease timeout: default (5m) → 10m so Helm waits long enough for
  the pod to become ready before rolling back

commit.message:
fix: restore detail rows after sort/filter — accordion now opens correctly (#44)

document.getElementById() cannot find detached DOM nodes. Both setupGroups
and filterAndSort removed all rows from the DOM before trying to look up
detail rows by ID, so detail rows were permanently lost after the first
sort or filter interaction.

Fix: build a Map<dataRow, detailRow> while elements are still attached,
then use that map for all subsequent look-ups in the same function.

Add a JSDOM-based regression test that:
- generates the dashboard HTML with two repos
- fires DOMContentLoaded (triggers setupGroups)
- changes the sort (triggers filterAndSort)
- clicks each expand button (toggleRepo)

Add Bicep infrastructure templates for Azure migration (#152)

* Add Bicep infrastructure templates for Azure migration

Modular Bicep templates to deploy the full Agora CMS stack to Azure:

- networking.bicep: VNet with subnets for Container Apps + PostgreSQL,
  private DNS zone for Postgres
- postgres.bicep: Azure Database for PostgreSQL Flexible Server (B1ms),
  SSL enforced, private networking, automated backups
- storage.bicep: Storage Account with Azure Files share (FFmpeg transcode
  workspace) and Blob containers (originals + variants for device delivery)
- acr.bicep: Azure Container Registry (Basic tier)
- keyVault.bicep: Key Vault with RBAC authorization for secrets management
- containerApps.bicep: Container Apps Environment with CMS app (WebSocket
  ingress, Azure Files mount, 1 vCPU/2GB) and MCP server (0.25 vCPU/0.5GB)
- main.bicep: Orchestrator wiring all modules together
- prod.bicepparam: Production parameter template

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add storage abstraction layer for local/Azure backends

Implement StorageBackend ABC with two implementations:
- LocalStorageBackend: uses local filesystem (no-op sync hooks, FileResponse)
- AzureStorageBackend: syncs files to Azure Blob Storage after writes/deletes,
  serves device downloads via time-limited SAS URLs

Integration points:
- config.py: AGORA_CMS_STORAGE_BACKEND (local|azure) + Azure credentials
- main.py: initializes backend at startup based on config
- routers/assets.py: cloud sync on upload/delete, storage-aware downloads
- routers/ws.py: device download URLs use SAS URLs in Azure mode
- services/transcoder.py: syncs completed variants to Blob Storage

The local filesystem path (Docker volume or Azure Files mount) remains the
primary I/O layer for FFmpeg, checksums, and probing. The Azure backend
adds cloud sync + SAS URL generation on top.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

The test app fixture uses a no-op lifespan (skipping the real startup

Initialize a LocalStorageBackend in the shared app fixture so all
tests have a working storage backend.

Convert vglist to API-only Rails + Vue 3 SPA architecture (#4377)

* Add conversion plan for Rails API-only + Vue 3/Vite 8 SPA

Documents the strategy for splitting the monolith into an API-only
Rails backend and a standalone Vue SPA frontend using GraphQL.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01Apnz6AoAeGn5bZpMPJSQK7

* Convert Rails app to API-only mode for Vue SPA frontend

This is Phase 1 of the conversion to a Rails API + Vue SPA architecture.

Backend changes:
- Set config.api_only = true, ApplicationController inherits ActionController::API
- Add JWT authentication (via jwt gem) for stateless SPA auth
- Add API auth endpoints: POST /api/auth/sign_in, sign_up, GET /api/auth/me
- Add health check endpoint: GET /health
- Update CORS to allow all requests from the frontend (configurable via FRONTEND_URL env)
- Enable Doorkeeper api_only mode
- Configure Devise for API mode (navigational_formats = [])
- Add session/cookie middleware back for Devise/Warden compatibility

New GraphQL mutations:
- signIn, signUp, requestPasswordReset (unauthenticated auth flow)
- updateUser (profile updates: bio, privacy, hide_days_played)
- resetApiToken (generate new API token)
- exportLibrary (export game library as JSON)
- GraphQL endpoint now allows unauthenticated access (schema handles auth per-mutation)

Removed (moved to future Vue SPA frontend):
- All ERB views, layouts, and partials
- All HTML-only controllers (home, activity, search, settings, admin, etc.)
- Frontend assets (JS, CSS/SCSS, SVG icons, images)
- Webpack config, package.json, yarn.lock, tsconfig.json
- View helpers (application, activity, games, users, settings)
- Gems: propshaft, jsbundling-rails, turbolinks, jbuilder, inline_svg, invisible_captcha, web-console, launchy, capybara, selenium-webdriver

Updated tests:
- Removed feature specs (Capybara/Selenium - will be replaced by Playwright in frontend)
- Removed request specs for deleted HTML controllers
- Updated graphql_spec.rb for new auth flow (unauthenticated access + JWT)
- Updated spec_helper.rb and rails_helper.rb to remove capybara/selenium references

https://claude.ai/code/session_01Apnz6AoAeGn5bZpMPJSQK7

* Add Vue 3 SPA frontend with Vite, Vue Router, Apollo Client, and Pinia

Phase 2 of the Rails API + Vue SPA conversion. Creates a standalone frontend
app in frontend/ with:
- Vite 6 build tooling with Vue 3, TypeScript strict mode
- Vue Router with lazy-loaded routes and auth navigation guards
- Apollo Client for GraphQL data fetching with JWT auth
- Pinia store for authentication state (localStorage persistence)
- All page components: games, users, platforms, companies, engines,
  genres, series, stores, settings, admin, search, activity
- NavBar with search dropdown using GLOBAL_SEARCH query
- Full SCSS theme ported from original app (Bulma 1.x with dark mode)
- Composables for auth flows (sign in, sign up, password reset)

https://claude.ai/code/session_01Apnz6AoAeGn5bZpMPJSQK7

* Upgrade to Vite 8 and @vitejs/plugin-vue 6

https://claude.ai/code/session_01Apnz6AoAeGn5bZpMPJSQK7

* Upgrade vue-router to 5.x, TypeScript to 5.9, pinia to 3.0.4, oxfmt to 0.40

Note: @apollo/client stays at 3.x because @vue/apollo-composable
requires ^3.4.13 and doesn't yet support Apollo Client 4.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01Apnz6AoAeGn5bZpMPJSQK7

* Replace Apollo Client with graphql-request for lighter GraphQL layer

Removes @apollo/client (~150kB gzip) and @vue/apollo-composable in favor
of graphql-request (~5kB). Creates thin useQuery/useMutation composables
that provide the same reactive API (data, loading, error, fetchMore, refetch).

Main bundle chunk drops from 43kB to 14kB gzipped. Build time drops from
2.6s to 1.8s.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01Apnz6AoAeGn5bZpMPJSQK7

* Phase 3: Update CI/CD, add SPA fallback route, and production deploy support

CI changes:
- Split frontend jobs (lint, typecheck, build) to run from frontend/ dir
- Remove rspec_features job (feature specs deleted in Phase 1)
- Remove root-level yarn install from backend-only jobs
- Use npx for graphql-schema-linter (no root package.json)

Production deployment:
- SpaController serves frontend/dist/index.html for client-side routing
- Catch-all route forwards unmatched HTML requests to the SPA
- Updated deploy.rake to build frontend and copy dist/ to public/

https://claude.ai/code/session_01Apnz6AoAeGn5bZpMPJSQK7

* Fix authentication bypass in GraphQL API token auth

The api_user method returned a user by email alone without verifying
the API token. The token validation result was stored but never checked,
allowing any request with just a valid X-User-Email header to
authenticate as that user.

Now requires both X-User-Email and X-User-Token headers, and only sets
@api_token_user when verify_api_token! returns true.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01Apnz6AoAeGn5bZpMPJSQK7

* Fix high-severity frontend issues from code review

- auth store: Wrap JSON.parse of localStorage in try/catch to prevent
  app crash from corrupted data
- GameShowPage: Add null-safe access to game.value?.name in async
  mutation callbacks, fix error casting
- UserShowPage: Add try/catch to follow/unfollow handlers, add
  :disabled binding to prevent double-click race conditions

https://claude.ai/code/session_01Apnz6AoAeGn5bZpMPJSQK7

* Require FRONTEND_URL env var in production for CORS

Previously fell back to http://localhost:5173 if FRONTEND_URL was unset,
which would be insecure in production. Now raises KeyError on boot if
the env var is missing in production.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01Apnz6AoAeGn5bZpMPJSQK7

* Reject banned users in JWT and API token auth paths

Previously, banned users could continue executing GraphQL mutations
via JWT or API token authentication because the ban check in
sign_out_banned_users only applies to Devise session-based auth.
Now both auth paths check user.banned? before setting the
authenticated user, treating banned users as unauthenticated.

commit.message:
Harden allocation algorithm against SELECT FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED contention (#9)

* Initial plan

* feat: retry allocation processor on SELECT FOR UPDATE lock contention

When findByIdsForUpdate returns fewer locked rows than expected (SKIP
LOCKED skipped rows held by concurrent transactions), the processor now
retries up to MAX_RETRIES=3 times with a 50ms back-off instead of
immediately marking the allocation as FAILED.

Key logic:
- hasContention = lockedCandidates.size < candidateIds.size
- Contention → retry; no contention but algorithm fails → fail immediately
- Phase 1 (findByState) is re-executed each attempt to reflect equipment
  committed by concurrent transactions (PostgreSQL READ COMMITTED)

Also updates AllocationProcessorTest:
- Replaces old single-attempt contention test with a MAX_RETRIES test
- Adds test for successful allocation on retry (contention resolves)
- Adds test for immediate failure without retrying on genuine shortage

* Refactor Kitty keyboard protocol detection & enablement

Update MainLoopCoordinator and driver to use the new API, and make KittyKeyboardCapabilities settable on IDriver. Update all usages and tests to use the new Flags property. Remove obsolete code and tests from AnsiOutput. Improve logging and tracing for Kitty protocol detection and driver initialization. Update Keys scenario and UICatalogRunnable to display Kitty protocol status using the new approach.

* Improve kitty keyboard protocol negotiation & cleanup

Enhances driver and main loop to reliably enable kitty keyboard mode on startup (with post-enable detection of actual flags) and disable it on shutdown before disposing output. Updates KittyKeyboardProtocolDetector to confirm and store enabled flags. Reduces debug output by commenting trace lines. Updates docs to clarify negotiation and shutdown flow. Adds/updates tests to verify enable/disable sequences and capability updates.

* Add tests for mixed Kitty and legacy input deduplication

Added a new test region to KittyKeyboardPipelineTests to ensure no duplicate KeyDown events are raised when both Kitty CSI-u sequences and legacy printable characters are received for the same keypress. Tests cover generic printable keys, Portuguese keyboard characters, and Kitty sequences with associated text, addressing terminal behaviors that emit both input types.

Added suppression logic in AnsiInputProcessor to prevent duplicate keydown events when both Kitty CSI-u and legacy printable sequences are received for the same key. Introduced virtual methods in InputProcessorImpl for customizable suppression. Updated related tests for style consistency.

fix: replace network_count proxy with HTML scraping for dependents

network_count from the GitHub REST API is the fork-network count,
not the dependents count. This caused forks (e.g. rajbos/microsoft-
todo-mcp-server) to show the upstream fork-network size (23) instead
of their real dependent count (0).

Changes:
- Return 0 immediately for fork repos (dependency graph is disabled
  for forks, so dependents is always 0)
- For non-forks, scrape /network/dependents?dependent_type=REPOSITORY
  and parse the count from the HTML tab link
- Guard against login-redirect pages (unauthenticated private repos)
- Forward GITHUB_TOKEN as Authorization header when available
- Add comprehensive tests covering forks, large counts with commas,
  same-line vs multi-line HTML, non-200 responses, and login redirects

- Add GitHubRateLimitError class and asRateLimitError() detector in client.ts
- fetchCommits/fetchPullRequests return partial results on rate limit
  instead of crashing the entire sync
- getFileChanges reports rate-limited commits instead of silently
  swallowing errors
- collectProjectData saves sync state in finally block so retries
  resume from where they left off
- collect/route.ts returns user-friendly rate limit message with
  retry guidance instead of generic error

Closes rate limit handling gaps where sync would crash, lose all
partial progress, and show a generic error.

commit.message:
fix: resolve bugs, performance, and security issues (#121)

Bugs
- models.py: add missing @router.get('/') decorator on list_models
- benchmarks.py: handle CSV uploads separately (was always calling json.loads)
- results.py: use safe_json_load for metadata_json to prevent 500s on bad rows

Performance
- results.py (export_csv): scope model/benchmark queries to campaign IDs, not full tables
- main.py (rate limiter): cap per-IP timestamp list to RPM entries to prevent unbounded growth

Security
- main.py: use hmac.compare_digest for constant-time API key comparison
- main.py: move hmac import to top-level imports
- models.py: validate endpoint URL scheme + resolve host to block SSRF (loopback, private, link-local)
- benchmarks.py: reject non-UTF-8 CSV with a clear 422 instead of silently mangling data
